Hotel Transylvania 3: Summer Vacation (2018)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $167,500,092Details
International Box Office $359,861,622
Worldwide Box Office $527,361,714
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $18,945,137
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $19,740,271
Total $38,685,408
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Welcome to Hotel Transylvania, Dracula’s lavish five-stake resort, where monsters and their families can live it up and no humans are allowed. One special weekend, Dracula has invited all his best friends—Frankenstein and his wife, the Mummy, the Invisible Man, the Werewolf family, and more—to celebrate his beloved daughter Mavis’s 118th birthday. For Dracula catering to all of these legendary monsters is no problem but the party really starts when one ordinary guy stumbles into the hotel and changes everything!

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$44,076,225 (26.3% of total gross)
Legs:3.80 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:31.8% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$65,000,000 (worldwide box office is 8.1 times production budget)
Theater counts:4,267 opening theaters/4,267 max. theaters, 6.4 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $219,901,332

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: July 13th, 2018 (Wide) by Sony Pictures
International Releases: June 22nd, 2018 (Wide), released as Hotel Transylvania 3: A Monster Vacation (Australia)
July 6th, 2018 (Wide), released as Hotel Transylvania 3: A Monster Vacation (New Zealand)
July 11th, 2018 (Wide) (Vietnam)
July 11th, 2018 (Wide) (Trinidad)
July 11th, 2018 (Wide) (Jamaica)
Video Release: October 9th, 2018 by Sony Pictures Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG for some action and rude humor
(Rating bulletin 2526 (Cert #50948), 5/9/2018)
Running Time: 97 minutes
Franchise: Hotel Transylvania
Film format: 3-D, 3-D - Shot in 3-D
Plot point: Dysfunctional Family, Faked Romance, Frankenstien's Monster, Generational Conflict, Interspecies Romance, Monster, Mummy, On a Boat, Romance, Vampire, Werewolf
Social setting: Summer Vacation
Subgenre: Kids Horror
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Adventure
Production Method:Digital Animation
Creative Type:Kids Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Sony Pictures Animation, Columbia Pictures
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

Welcome to Hotel Transylvania, Dracula’s lavish five-stake resort, where monsters and their families can live it up and no humans are allowed. One special weekend, Dracula has invited all his best friends—Frankenstein and his wife, the Mummy, the Invisible Man, the Werewolf family, and more—to celebrate his beloved daughter Mavis’s 118th birthday. For Dracula catering to all of these legendary monsters is no problem but the party really starts when one ordinary guy stumbles into the hotel and changes everything!
